data.title: Oil Wavers as Uncertainty Over War Remains — WSJ
data.description:
By Giulia Petroni and Angus Berwick

Oil prices fell overnight, with the global benchmark Brent crude briefly slipping below $100 a barrel after President Trump signaled he wants to wind down the war in Iran soon.

Later on Wednesday, though, Brent crude clawed back its losses to trade close to $104, as Iran cast shade on Trump's optimism and uncertainty remained over when energy flows in the Persian Gulf could return to normal.

Here's what analysts are saying:

  • "The market is caught between the largest supply disruption in modern history and growing de-escalation rhetoric from Washington," said Neil Crosby, associate vice president of oil analytics at Sparta Commodities. "Even if a ceasefire materialises, restoring supply takes weeks to months-damaged facilities need repair, the cargo backlog is enormous," he said, adding that the Strait of Hormuz reopening is far from guaranteed.
  • While crude benchmarks eased overnight, the physical market better reflects the supply shock. Analysts at Saxo Bank said buyers of Brent cargoes were still paying a premium of around $9 a barrel above the front-month futures price, which underscored "ongoing supply stress."
  • Analysts at Norwegian investment bank DNB Carnegie estimated that total oil flows through Hormuz are at about a quarter of their pre-war levels, taking into account the re-routing of some flows via Saudi Arabi's Red Sea port. "Consequently, we remain at extremely high disruption levels of oil flow from the Middle East," they said in a note.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Euro Bank Bonds Supply Declines in March Given Middle East Conflict — Market Talk
data.description: Issuance of new euro bank bonds slowed in March as debt issuers stayed cautious due to the Middle East war, ING's Marine Leleux says in a note. New bank bond supply amounted to 17.3 billion euros in March ($20 billion), about half of the total new supply in March 2025 and a third of February's level, Leleux says. The largest share of new supply was in covered bonds in March, she says. Covered bonds are debt securities issued by financial institutions and are backed by a pool of assets such as mortgages or public loans. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: ZAWYA: Standard Chartered Foundation and Village Capital launch Women in Tech 7 in Bahrain
data.description:
Bahrain – Standard Chartered Foundation, in partnership with Village Capital and Bahrain FinTech Bay, today announced the launch of the seventh cohort of the Women in Tech Bahrain Accelerator, reaffirming its long-term commitment to Bahrain’s innovation economy and confidence in the Kingdom’s future.

At a time of continued geopolitical and economic uncertainty, this initiative underscores Standard Chartered’s commitment to Bahrain, building on its track record, with a focus on supporting entrepreneurship, innovation and national talent.

The initiative is part of the Standard Chartered Foundation’s global programme, which supports women-led tech startups through tailored training, catalytic funding, and access to world-class networks. Bahrain FinTech Bay will deliver the programme locally, leveraging its strong track record in entrepreneur support and ecosystem development.

Through the initiative, selected startups will have access to up to BHD 28,000 (USD 75,000) in equity-free funding from the Standard Chartered Foundation, along with three months’ residence at Bahrain FinTech Bay. Since its launch, the Women in Tech accelerator in Bahrain has supported 61 startups through mentorship, with 22 receiving a combined BHD 145,367 (USD 385,000) in funding to help scale their ventures.

Dr. Boutros Klink, Chief Executive Officer & Head of Coverage, Bahrain, Standard Chartered, said: “Launching the seventh cohort of Women in Tech Bahrain is a strong expression of our confidence in Bahrain, its future, and the opportunities being created across its innovation economy. At a time when global conditions remain challenging, we believe it is more important than ever to back entrepreneurship, support national talent, and invest in initiatives that can help build long-term economic resilience.”

He added “For more than a century, Standard Chartered has stood alongside Bahrain’s progress. That commitment is unwavering. Through partnerships like this, we continue to invest in entrepreneurship, strengthen the innovation ecosystem, and support sustainable growth for the Kingdom.”

With women entrepreneurs continuing to face systemic barriers to scaling their businesses, the initiative is designed to help them overcome those challenges. Through the accelerator, women founders will benefit from tailored investment-readiness support, grant funding, and mentorship opportunities that will enable their ventures to grow sustainably and create long-lasting community impact.

Nakami Walunywa, Regional Director, Africa and Middle East at Village Capital said: “In 2025, 71 women-led startups in the programme grew their businesses and collectively generated over USD 2 million in additional revenue. This demonstrates that when founders have access to structured, locally-embedded support and catalytic funding, they can strengthen their strategies, engage investors confidently, and unlock sustainable impact. In 2026, we’re continuing to create the conditions for even more women-led startups to thrive and positively impact their communities.”

From his end, Bader Sater, CEO of Bahrain FinTech Bay, under which Innovate for Bahrain operates, commented: "Reaching the seventh cohort of this programme is something we are genuinely proud of. Seven cohorts in, and the ambition we see from Bahraini women in tech has only grown stronger. Year after year, we see founders walk in with bold ideas and leave ready to compete, and that is exactly what this initiative set out to do. This programme was built on the belief that the right support at the right time can change a startup's trajectory, and we have seen that proven time and again. We are glad to be delivering this once again through Innovate for Bahrain, in partnership with Standard Chartered and our strategic partners, and we cannot wait to see the ideas, the energy, and the stories this cohort will bring.”

Women startup founders from Bahrain are invited to apply, with applications closing on 6 June 2026. Successful applicants will participate in the Accelerator between June and August 2026.

Further information

Media contact

Noora Al Nusuf

Head, Corporate Affairs and Brand & Marketing, Bahrain and

Islamic Banking Communications

Standard Chartered Bank

Phone: +973 17150760

Email:   Noora.alnusuf@sc.com

About Standard Chartered 

We are a leading international banking group, with a presence in 53 of the world’s most dynamic markets. Our purpose is to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, and our heritage and values are expressed in our brand promise, here for good.

Standard Chartered PLC is listed on the London and Hong Kong stock exchanges. For more stories and expert opinions please visit https://www.sc.com/en/insights/Insights at sc.com. Follow Standard Chartered on X, LinkedIn, Instagram and Facebook.

About Standard Chartered Foundation    

The Standard Chartered Foundation is a charitable organisation established by Standard Chartered in 2019. It is committed to helping tackle inequality by empowering under-served young people, especially women and people with disabilities. It sets the strategy for - and funds - youth employability and entrepreneurship programmes across Standard Chartered’s markets, helping young people access the skills, networks and support they need to secure decent work and grow their businesses. The Standard Chartered Foundation is a registered charity in England and Wales (charity number 1184946). See the website for more information: https://www.sc.com/en/about/investing-in-communities/scfoundation/Standard Chartered Foundation.

About Village Capital

Village Capital is a global leader in impact investing, helping early-stage startups access the type of social and financial capital they need to grow – reshaping the supply and demand sides of capital flows as a result.

Since 2009, Village Capital has directly supported more than 2,100 impact startups in 70 countries. Together, these founders have raised over $9.6B across economic mobility, environmental sustainability, and access to essential services. It has made 116 investments through its various affiliated funds, including Vilcap Investments, which has invested in 91 peer-selected companies. Additionally, 26,000 entrepreneurs have used its Abaca web app to take at least one assessment, helping them expand their access to financial capital.

Learn more at www.vilcap.com and follow @villagecapital.

About Bahrain FinTech Bay (BFB):

Bahrain FinTech Bay (BFB) is the leading fintech hub in the Kingdom of Bahrain and the region's gateway for financial technology innovation. We incubate impactful and scalable fintech initiatives through innovation labs, acceleration programs, curated activities, and educational programs. Our mandate is to attract international fintech players to establish a presence in Bahrain, support the local ecosystem by driving sustainable growth within the sector, and cultivate talent through education and the adoption of essential finance and technology skills across the MENA region.

Bahrain FinTech Bay is a subsidiary of The BENEFIT Company.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Palm Oil Ends Lower on Likely Profit-Taking — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil futures closed lower, with the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for June delivery falling 60 ringgit to 4,768 ringgit a metric ton. The futures likely fell on profit-taking activities after a recent rally, Kenanga Futures analysts say in a note. This is despite overnight gains in rival soy oils and continued optimism surrounding Indonesia's advancing biofuel mandate, they add. The analysts peg the support levels for the June contract at 4,780 ringgit and resistance levels at 4,950 ringgit. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

data.title: Synergy CHC 2025 10-K: Revenue $30.4M, Net Loss $(12.34)M
data.description:
Synergy CHC reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $30.38 million and a net loss of $12.34 million, a swing from net income of $2.12 million in 2024 driven by lower sales, higher expenses, and receivable write-offs. Gross profit was $20.30 million, or 67% of revenue, modestly down versus the prior year. The results reflect reduced nutraceutical and beverage-related sales alongside operational and licensing adjustments during the year.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $30.38 million (declined from $34.83 million in 2024).
  • Gross Profit: $20.30 million (67% of revenue; down from $23.64 million, 68% in 2024).
  • Net Income: $(12.34) million, compared with $2.12 million in 2024 (turn to loss due to lower revenue, higher expenses, and receivable write-offs).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Reported revenue declined approximately 13% year-over-year to $30.4 million in 2025, driven primarily by lower nutraceutical sales and reduced beverage testing activity.
  • Channel & Product Shift: Management shifted focus toward developing beverage SKUs and reduced emphasis on core nutraceuticals during 2025.
  • Brand & IP Activity: Core brands FOCUSfactor and Flat Tummy remained central to the business; the company engaged in IP licensing activity but subsequently canceled and reversed a $2.9 million license.
  • Product Launches & Market Development: Launched a ready-to-drink FOCUSfactor beverage and expanded market development efforts around beverage offerings.
  • Workforce Actions: Implemented cost reductions by laying off 13 employees as part of rightsizing efforts.

Original SEC Filing: Synergy CHC Corp. [ SNYR ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Osprey Bitcoin Trust 2025 10-K: Net loss $13.45M, NAV decline; EPS impact reflected in trust operations
data.description:
Osprey Bitcoin Trust reported a net decrease in net assets resulting from operations of $13.45 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven primarily by losses on its Bitcoin holdings and redemptions that reduced managed assets. The trust recorded a net realized and unrealized loss on its investment in Bitcoin of $11.32 million (comprised of $31.71 million realized gain and $43.02 million unrealized depreciation) and incurred a management fee of $0.95 million paid in USD. These results contributed to a 24% reduction in net assets to $136.7 million during 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income / Net decrease in net assets from operations: $(13.45) million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025.
  • Net realized and unrealized gain (loss) on investment in Bitcoin: $(11.32) million for 2025, composed of a $31.71 million realized gain and $43.02 million unrealized depreciation.
  • Management fee: $0.95 million for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 (paid in USD and included in operations).
  • Net assets: Declined 24% in 2025 to $136.7 million (reflecting market movement in Bitcoin and redemptions).

Business Highlights

  • NAV and asset movement: Net assets fell 24% in 2025 to $136.7 million, driven by the decline in Bitcoin prices and approximately $30.6 million in investor redemptions.
  • Market sensitivity: Operating results and NAV are highly correlated with Bitcoin volatility; the trust experienced material swings in NAV and share pricing as a result.
  • Expense and fee management: The sponsor covers routine operating expenses; the management fee is paid in USD and funded via Bitcoin sales when necessary.
  • Market access and listing: Shares moved from OTCQX to a primary exchange listing on December 19, 2025, which narrowed the trust's historical premium/discount to NAV.
  • Liquidity mechanisms: The trust employs short-term Trade Credits to provide settlement flexibility; repayment of Trade Credits is required in the same asset type (BTC or cash).

Original SEC Filing: Osprey Bitcoin Trust [ OBTC ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: OLB Group 2025 10-K: Revenue $8.68M, EPS (1.74)
data.description:
OLB Group reported 2025 revenue of $8.68 million and a net loss per common share of $(1.74), with overall net loss improving versus the prior year largely due to lower operating expenses and reduced impairments. The company generated an operating loss of $(5.17) million and a net loss of $(5.87) million for the year, reflecting continued revenue pressure from its digital products and processing services. Management highlights operational actions including vendor transitions and a planned spin-off that are intended to improve margins and positioning going forward.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $8.68 million, decreased 32.4% versus 2024, driven primarily by lower Moola Cloud digital product and processing revenue.
  • Operating income (loss): $(5.17) million, an improvement from the prior year operating loss of $(11.68) million, driven by lower impairment, depreciation and general & administrative expenses.
  • Net loss: $(5.87) million, improved by $5.35 million versus 2024, principally from reduced operating expenses and impairments.
  • Net loss applicable to common stockholders: $(6.68) million, which includes a $775,000 deemed dividend on preferred stock.
  • Net loss per common share, basic and diluted: $(1.74); weighted average shares outstanding: 3.84 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Revenue declined 32% year-over-year to $8.68 million in 2025, attributed to lower transaction, subscription, and digital product sales within Moola Cloud.
  • Channel & vendor transition: The company is transitioning Moola Cloud vendors to improve pricing; the majority of vendor transitions are expected to be completed by Q1 2026.
  • Merchant processing scale: eVance processes more than $100 million and approximately 1.4 million transactions monthly; SecurePay is used by roughly 3,000 merchants, representing about $9 million gross merchandise volume per month.
  • Product and segment developments: Highlights include the 2019 launch of instant merchant/ISO onboarding and the formation of subsidiaries OLBit (money transmission) and DMINT (Bitcoin mining).
  • Operational actions and outlook: Management reduced amortization/depreciation and recorded asset impairments, leading to lower operating expenses; the company is pursuing a spin-off of DMINT pending S-1 filing and Nasdaq approval, which may affect future operational structure and focus.

Original SEC Filing: OLB GROUP, INC. [ OLB ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: NewHold Investment III reports $4.92M net income, $0.20 EPS — 10-K summary
data.description:
NewHold Investment III reported net income of $4.918 million for the period, driven primarily by interest income on its trust account while operating activities produced a loss. The company recorded net income per share of $0.20 for both Class A and Class B ordinary shares. Revenue to date consists of interest income rather than operating sales, reflecting the company's blank-check structure.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: $4.918 million, primarily from interest income on the Trust Account of $7.008 million offset by general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: Class A ordinary shares – basic and diluted: $0.20; Class B ordinary shares – basic and diluted: $0.20.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(2.09) million, reflecting general and administrative costs of $2.09 million.

Business Highlights

  • IPO and Funding: Completed an initial public offering and private placement, with proceeds placed in a Trust Account to fund an initial business combination.
  • Operating Activity: No operating revenues to date; corporate activities have been limited to organization, the offering process, and searching for a target business combination.
  • Search and Transaction Efforts: Actively identifying potential targets and incurring due diligence and transaction-related costs associated with completing a business combination.
  • Cash Management: Trust Account assets are invested in short-term U.S. government instruments; interest income is expected to cover taxes and support ongoing search operations.

Original SEC Filing: NewHold Investment Corp. III [ NHIC ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Forum Markets 2025 10-K: $6.55M Revenue, Loss From Operations $233.45M, Net Loss $450.52M
data.description:
Forum Markets reported $6.547 million in total revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven by staking and incentive activity following a strategic shift into digital-asset services. The company recorded a loss from operations of $233.450 million and a net loss of $450.521 million for the same period, reflecting substantial charges tied to its transition and investments. Earnings per share figures were not provided in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $6.547 million (reflecting staking and incentive activity after strategic transition).
  • Operating Income (Loss From Operations): $(233.450) million.
  • Net Income (Net Loss): $(450.521) million for the year ended December 31, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Strategic pivot: Transitioned business focus from biotech/gaming to digital-asset treasury, staking, investment and tokenization activities.
  • Rebranding and market identity: Rebranded to ETHZilla in August 2025 and later to Forum Markets in March 2026; Nasdaq ticker changed to FRMM.
  • Strategic investments and acquisitions: Closed investments in Satschel, Karus and Zippy; completed Aero Token offering and pursued engine/loan acquisitions tied to tokenization initiatives.
  • Tokenization initiatives: Launched manufactured-home loan and aircraft-engine tokenization programs and is pursuing Liquidity.io distribution to scale token offerings.
  • Operational focus: Emphasis on building a digital-asset treasury and staking infrastructure alongside tokenization products to drive future growth and liquidity strategies.

Original SEC Filing: FORUM MARKETS Inc [ FRMM ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: D. Boral Acquisition I 2025 10-K: Net Loss $(66,845), EPS $(0.01)
data.description:
D. Boral Acquisition I reported a net loss of $66,845 for the period Apr 3–Dec 31, 2025, reflecting formation and operating costs with no revenue recorded. Cash on hand at period end totaled $0.025 million, primarily used for target identification and due diligence, and basic net loss per Class B ordinary share was $(0.01). The company has not commenced operations and continues to focus on completing an initial business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income (Loss): $(66,845) for the period Apr 3–Dec 31, 2025, reflecting formation and operating costs.
  • Net Loss Per Share, Class B ordinary shares: $(0.01) basic net loss per share for the period.
  • Cash: $0.025 million at period end, used for identifying and evaluating targets and due diligence.

Business Highlights

  • Operational Status: The company has not commenced operations and expects no revenue until after an initial business combination.
  • Capital Deployment Focus: Approximately $864,000 held outside the trust is being allocated to target identification, due diligence, travel and transaction structuring.
  • Business Combination Strategy: Primary objective is completing an initial business combination; sponsor and officers may provide bridge loans that could be convertible into units to facilitate transactions.
  • Risk & Outlook: Management is focused on selecting targets amid geopolitical and market volatility, which could delay or affect the timing and terms of a potential transaction.

Original SEC Filing: D. Boral Acquisition I Corp. [ DBCA ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

data.title: Asiafin Holdings 2025 10-K: Revenue $5.13M, Improved EPS and Narrowed Net Loss
data.description:
Asiafin Holdings reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $5.13 million, a 51.6% increase year-over-year driven by higher IT services sales, and recorded a net loss attributable to common shareholders of $85,333, an improvement from $144,000 in 2024. Gross profit was $1.90 million (37.1% margin) and operating income turned positive to $39,670 versus an operating loss in the prior year. Operating cash flow improved materially to $503,858, supporting increased working capital needs as the company scaled operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $5.13 million, up 51.6% vs 2024, driven by higher IT services sales (notably Saudi Arabia).
  • Gross Profit: $1.90 million, representing a 37.1% gross margin, up $0.48 million (33.7%) vs 2024.
  • Income/(loss) from operations: $39,670, a swing to positive from a $(42,980) operating loss in 2024 (192.3% improvement).
  • Net loss attributable to common shareholders: $(85,333), improved 40.6% vs prior year net loss of $(144,000).
  • Operating cash flow: $503,858, a material improvement supporting working capital.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth engine: Increased IT services sales, with notable expansion of business from Saudi Arabia, drove the year-over-year revenue gain.
  • Channel and customer momentum: Expanded client base in Asia; SFHL is serving more than 90 banks and RPA solutions have been deployed to over 100 customers.
  • Operational scaling: The company hired additional staff to support growth, which increased SG&A (notably salaries) as operations scaled.
  • Cash & working capital: Improved operating cash flow provided liquidity to support higher working capital requirements associated with growth.

Original SEC Filing: ASIAFIN HOLDINGS CORP. [ ASFH ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Amaze Holdings 10-K: Revenue $1.97M, EPS $(8.18)
data.description:
Amaze Holdings reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $1.967 million and a net loss of $(55.165) million, or $(8.18) per share, driven by the March 2025 acquisition of Amaze Software which materially increased platform transaction volume. Revenue rose sharply year-over-year while operating results were impacted by significant goodwill impairment and amortization charges. The results reflect a transition toward an asset-light, creator-focused platform model with higher platform margins but substantial one-time and operating expenses.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $1.967 million, an increase of 558% versus 2024 driven by the Amaze acquisition.
  • Gross income (loss): $1.571 million, improved from a $(5,819) loss in 2024 due to platform margins.
  • Loss from operations: $(54.393) million, reflecting $15.7 million of SG&A, $3.347 million of amortization, and a $34.295 million goodwill impairment.
  • Net loss: $(55.165) million, compared to $(2.519) million in 2024.
  • Net loss per share: $(8.18) per share, versus $(3.83) per share in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: E-commerce became the dominant channel, accounting for 71% of revenue as the company shifts focus toward creator-platform transactions and away from DTC and wholesale.
  • Acquisition and integration: Completed acquisition of Amaze Software on March 7, 2025; integration efforts included onboarding multichannel suppliers and embedding platform technology.
  • Marketing and distribution strategy: Emphasis on social-first marketing and creator partnerships to build brand awareness and retain fan data for targeted re‑engagement.
  • Operational model: Adopted an asset-light approach with expansion of in-country suppliers to improve margins and scalability while minimizing inventory risk.
  • Platform efficiency: Increased platform transaction volume contributed to revenue growth and improved platform-level margins.

Original SEC Filing: AMAZE HOLDINGS, INC. [ AMZE ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: UK equities climb 1% as Trump signals end to Iran war
data.description:
London's main indexes climbed on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump signalled that the Iran war could end soon, prompting investors to scale back expectations for further interest rate hikes by the Bank of England.

The blue-chip FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 rose 1.6% at 1044 GMT, while the midcap FTSE 250 climbed 1.8%. On Tuesday, both indexes marked their biggest monthly drop since 2020 on fears that the war-led increase in oil prices will stoke inflation.

  • Trump and his Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the end of the war on Iran could be near, with Washington signalling potential for both direct talks with Tehran's leadership and a winding down of the conflict even without a deal.

  • Interest rate futures were fully pricing in one 25 basis-point increase in the BoE's bank rate by the end of 2026, and the possibility of a second compared with two or three hikes.

  • Most FTSE 350 sub-sectors traded higher, except energy , which fell 1% after oil slid more than 3% amid Middle East volatility.

  • Heavyweight banks (.FTNMX301010) rose 3.4%, providing the biggest boost to the benchmark index, with Standard Chartered , Lloyds Banking Group and Barclays up between 3.4% and 4.1%.

  • Precious metal miners rose 4.7%, as gold rose on de-escalation hopes.

  • Britain's food prices will be rising by almost 10% by the end of 2026 due to the Iran war, the country's food and drink manufacturers' lobby warned, around three times faster than its previous forecast.

  • British factory cost pressures soared in March, and delivery delays - due to ships avoiding the Strait of Hormuz - were the longest since mid-2022, according to an S&P Global survey.

  • Berkeley fell 15.1% after the home builder forecast that profit growth would slow through 2030. The firm said it would halt land purchases as the war and the risk of higher interest rates dampen hopes of a housing market recovery.

data.title: ZAWYA: Alteryx expands regional leadership with Sabya Sen to lead IMEA & APAC
data.description:
Alteryx, Inc., a leading AI-ready data and analytics company, today announced the appointment of Sabya Sen as Vice President, IMEA & APAC, to lead its business across India, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific (IMEA & APAC). In this role, Sen will focus on accelerating customer outcomes and scaling adoption of the Alteryx One platform across some of the world’s fastest-growing markets for AI and data innovation.

Sen brings deep regional expertise and a strong track record of execution to this role. Most recently, he served as Vice President, Head of UKI & Emerging Markets Europe at Alteryx, where he drove consistent growth, built high-performing teams, and strengthened customer relationships across global markets.

The appointment comes at a critical phase for AI adoption globally and across IMEA and APAC, where governments and enterprises are increasing investments in digital transformation. In the Middle East, national strategies like Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 and UAE's We the UAE 2031 are accelerating innovation agendas. The region’s AI market is projected to reach $320 billion USD, while globally, 89% of leaders plan to maintain or increase AI budgets in 2026. Despite this momentum, many organizations remain in pilot phases, underscoring the need for scalable, governed analytics solutions that can support enterprise-wide AI adoption.

“It is my priority to continue delivering meaningful outcomes for our customers and community,” said Sabya Sen, Vice President, IMEA & APAC. “Across the Middle East, India, and Asia-Pacific, we are seeing unprecedented momentum driven by ambitious national transformation agendas. These efforts are accelerating economic diversification, advancing digital innovation, and firmly positioning these regions as global hubs for data and AI-led growth.”

Prior to joining Alteryx, Sen spent 11 years at Salesforce in a variety of leadership roles, supporting customers across the insurance, financial services, and healthcare industries, where he helped organizations leverage data and technology to transform their operations. In his new role, Sen will focus on helping organizations move beyond experimentation to enterprise-scale AI adoption by delivering trusted, AI-ready data and analytics through the Alteryx One platform.

"Over the past few years, Sabya has had a tremendous impact on Alteryx and has demonstrated a remarkable level of focus, discipline, and strong commercial execution," said Jason Janicke, Senior Vice President, EMEA & APJ at Alteryx. "He has delivered results, built a strong team culture, and consistently raised the bar. We can't wait to see the impact that Sabya has in this next role."

ABOUT ALTERYX

Alteryx is a leading AI-ready data and analytics company that powers actionable insights to help organizations drive smarter, faster decisions with data. More than 8,000 customers around the world rely on Alteryx to automate analytics, improve revenue performance, manage costs, and mitigate risk across their businesses.  

Alteryx is a registered trademark of Alteryx, Inc. All other product and brand names may be trademarks or registered trademarks of their respective owners.

data.title: Middle East Crude-Benchmarks decline amid light trading; oil futures slide
data.description:
Middle East crude benchmark spot premiums Oman, Murban and Dubai declined on Wednesday amid thin trading, following a drop in oil futures.

Oil reversed earlier gains on Wednesday as uncertainty over the situation in the Middle East unnerved markets and U.S. President Donald Trump again suggested the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran could be nearing an end.

Murban and Oman futures also dropped on Wednesday.

Saudi Arabia may hike its May crude official selling prices (OSP) to Asia to record highs as Middle East oil became the world's most expensive after the war caused massive supply disruptions, according to a Reuters survey of industry sources.

The May OSP for flagship Arab Light crude, the country's most widely sold grade, may rise to premiums of $22.50 to $40.50 a barrel above the average of Dubai and Oman quotes, six sources said in the survey, $20-$38 a barrel higher than the OSP for April.

The wide range of Saudi oil prices reflects deep uncertainty among Asian buyers following severe price volatility as the war has limited shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint for a fifth of the world's oil supplies.

It remains unclear when a ceasefire will be reached or when oil exports from Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Iraq, Kuwait, Qatar and Bahrain can resume through the Strait.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps fell $1.23 to $19.80 a barrel.

NEWS

John Addison, Vitol's top U.S. gasoline trader who helped the firm win a deal for the first exports of Venezuelan oil following leader Nicolas Maduro's capture by U.S. forces, plans to retire from the world's largest commodities trading house, four sources told Reuters on Tuesday.

U.S. crude oil output fell by the most in two years during January following a severe winter storm that knocked production offline in large swathes of the country, data from the Energy Information Administration showed on Tuesday.

Brazilian fuel oil imports in Southeast Asia jumped in March, shipping data showed, easing concerns of tight marine fuel supply this month after the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran crimped shipments from the Middle East.

Indian fuel retailers raised jet fuel and commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) prices on Wednesday, following a sharp spike in global prices due to the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

data.title: Charlie's Holdings posts $20.9M revenue and $4.5M net income on 10-K
data.description:
Charlie's Holdings reported $20.916 million in product revenue for the year, driven by strong sales of SBX and nicotine alternatives, and recorded net income of $4.499 million largely reflecting a $7.5 million gain on the sale of intellectual property. Gross profit was $5.655 million (approximately a 27% margin), while loss from operations narrowed to $(2.165) million. The company highlighted substantial top-line growth and operational initiatives that supported its market expansion.

Financial Highlights

  • Product revenue, net: $20.916 million, increased $13.151 million (169.4%) year-over-year driven by SBX and nicotine alternatives.
  • Gross profit: $5.655 million, representing roughly a 27% margin of revenue (COGS ~73%), up $2.778 million (96.6%) year-over-year.
  • Loss from operations: $(2.165) million, improved $1.139 million (‑34.5%) versus the prior year.
  • Net income: $4.499 million, a positive swing of $8.658 million versus the prior-year loss, due largely to a $7.5 million gain on sale of IP.
  • Net income per share: Metric omitted per source instructions.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and product drivers: Significant year-over-year revenue increase driven by SBX non-nicotine and nicotine product sales and strong consumer preference for SBX, PACHA and Pachamama brands.
  • Channel expansion: Major expansion into chain convenience stores, anchored by a $4.4 million SBX order placed at NACS that is enabling a mass-market rollout.
  • Production and manufacturing: Opened a U.S. manufacturing facility and launched new disposable product lines with 25K and 75K-puff capacities.
  • Regulatory and compliance strategy: Implemented an IKE age-gating partnership and pursued PMTA asset monetization to support compliance-led growth and market access.
  • Brand momentum: Brands gaining state approvals and showing improved competitive positioning in consumer preference versus peers.

Original SEC Filing: Charlie's Holdings, Inc. [ CHUC ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Capstone Companies 2025 10-K: Revenue $0, Net Loss $(920,168)
data.description:
Capstone Companies reported zero revenue for the year ended 2025 and a net loss of $(920,168), reflecting the cessation of its operating product line and limited recurring sales; gross profit is $0 and operating loss totaled $(1,070,894). The company cited the absence of product revenues after 2024 and recognized impairments and ongoing general and administrative costs that contributed to the loss. Management is pursuing strategic transactions and partnerships to restore a revenue-generating business line.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue, Net: $0, decreased 100% from $143,269 in 2024 due to no operating product line.
  • Gross Profit (Loss): $0, versus $105,250 in 2024 (73.4% of net revenues) reflecting no sales.
  • Operating Loss: $(1,070,894), comparable to $(995,815) in 2024, driven by impairment and general & administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(920,168), versus $(962,384) in 2024 after tax benefits of $195,958.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not presented in Part II Item 7/Item 8 and therefore omitted.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: 2025 revenue fell to $0 after 2024 product sales, reflecting loss of the operating product line and halted product revenues.
  • Product Strategy Shift: Management pivoted away from Connected Surfaces/Smart Mirrors toward pursuing licensing for Connected Chef and building a new commercial/foodservice (HFS) industry business line.
  • Licensing & Production Milestones: The company secured a 2025 licensee for Connected Chef but subsequently lost that licensee due to OEM production term failures; relaunch efforts are paused.
  • Operational Cost Management: Headcount reductions and cuts to sales, marketing, and consulting were implemented to preserve cash while maintaining essential operations.
  • Strategic Development Focus: Actively seeking a strategic partner or transaction — including discussions about e-bike opportunities with eBliss — to establish a new revenue-generating business line.

Original SEC Filing: CAPSTONE COMPANIES, INC. [ CAPC ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Viscom: EBIT loss narrowed in 2025 as order intake grew, with 2026 guidance targeting profitability
data.description:
Order intake rose 7.9% while revenue declined 2.8% in 2025, with EBIT loss narrowing sharply. Growth in Asia and battery inspection offset declines in Europe and America. 2026 guidance anticipates revenue of €80–90M and a return to positive EBIT.

Original document: Viscom AG [V6C] Slides Release — Apr. 1 2026
